lead:
**Thoshan Flash** is a fine-tuned version of Google's **Gemma-2-9B** base model, published on Hugging Face with a live Gradio inference interface. This is explicitly **not** a model built from scratch — it is a parameter-efficient fine-tune of an existing pre-trained model using QLoRA (Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation), which reduces GPU memory requirements from ~36 GB to ~12 GB.

The project achieved 26+ downloads in its first month on the Hugging Face Hub. It was also connected to the CHIAC ASI internship — the ML techniques used in the fine-tuning (attention mechanisms, transformer internals, PEFT methodology) formed part of the Phase 2 research study.

content.content:
Full fine-tuning of a 9B-parameter model requires approximately 36 GB of GPU memory — far beyond what consumer hardware provides. QLoRA solves this with a three-part strategy:

**Step 1:** Load the base model in 4-bit quantized form, which dramatically reduces memory footprint while preserving most of the model's learned representations.

**Step 2:** Inject small trainable **LoRA adapter layers** at key weight matrices within the transformer. These adapters are the only parts that get updated during training.

**Step 3:** At export, merge the trained adapter weights back into the base model weights, producing a single fine-tuned model file ready for inference.

content.title: Parameter efficiency
content.content: LoRA adapters typically represent only 1–3% of the total model parameters. Training 1% of a 9B-parameter model is vastly more resource-efficient than training all 9B parameters — without sacrificing significant capability on the target task.

rows.Detail: Gemma-2-9B (Google) — pre-trained on large text corpus
4-bit → reduced VRAM from ~36 GB to ~12 GB

content.content:
After training, the model was uploaded to the Hugging Face Hub under the username `lingadevaruhp`. A Gradio inference interface was deployed alongside it, allowing anyone to run inference without local setup.

Within the first month of publication, the model received **26+ downloads** — demonstrating that it was being pulled and used by people beyond the original creator.

The Hugging Face model card documents the fine-tuning setup, hyperparameters, and intended use case, following the standard model documentation practice.

content.title: Live inference
content.content: A Gradio interface is deployed at go.lingadevaru.in/LLM and on the Hugging Face model page. No local installation required to try the model.

Thoshan Flash is deliberately framed with accuracy: it is a fine-tune of an existing model, not a model built from scratch. This honest framing is part of a broader approach to self-presentation — distinguishing what was actually built versus what was just used or studied.

The fine-tuning work connects to the [[about|skill profile]] in AI/ML: parameter-efficient fine-tuning, Hugging Face ecosystem, and deployment of ML models to public inference endpoints. It does not connect to software development or model architecture design.

For other ML work, see [[fraud-detection|Fraud Detection System]] — a traditional ML ensemble built in C# for banking fraud classification.
